name: Crush
description: Charm's open-source terminal coding agent (Go); multi-provider with LSP and MCP awareness.

developer.comment: Built by Charm (charmbracelet); originally created by Kujtim Hoxha and brought under Charm.

first-release.comment: First public release / announcement late July 2025 (announced 2025-07-30).

status.comment: Actively developed (v0.4.x line as of check 2026-06-05); pre-1.0 but maintained.

license.comment: Licensed FSL-1.1-MIT (Functional Source License): source-available, converts to MIT after 2 years. Marketed as 'open-source' but FSL is not OSI-approved, so tagged source-available per RESEARCH.md.

specialization.comment: Broad terminal coding agent; not optimized for a narrower niche.

interface.comment: Terminal TUI coding agent; runs in any terminal across macOS, Linux, Windows, BSD.

host-ides.comment: Runs in the terminal; not an IDE extension or standalone editor.

open-source.comment: Source is public but under FSL-1.1-MIT (source-available, MIT after 2 years), not an OSI-approved open license at release; marked false per RESEARCH.md guidance.

default-model-provider.comment: No single default vendor; supports Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, Groq, OpenRouter, Bedrock, and 30+ providers, with mid-session switching.

model-choice.comment: Switch among many models/providers, including mid-session model switching that preserves context.

bring-your-own-key.comment: Requires the user's own provider API keys; also supports custom OpenAI-/Anthropic-compatible endpoints.

local-model-support.comment: Local models via Ollama and LM Studio (OpenAI-compatible endpoints).

multi-file-editing.comment: File editing and multi-file operations within a session.

autonomous-task-execution.comment: Agentic loop: edits files and runs shell/build/test commands toward a goal; --yolo can run without per-action approval.

terminal-command-execution.comment: Runs shell commands as part of its loop, gated by permission prompts (allowed_tools / --yolo to bypass).

mcp-support.comment: MCP servers via stdio, HTTP, and SSE transports.

subagents-parallel-agents.comment: No documented subagent/parallel-agent orchestration as of check 2026-06-05; sessions are single linear loops.

codebase-indexing.comment: Uses LSP for context and reads files on demand; no semantic embedding index documented (LSP retrieval is not indexing per RESEARCH.md).

planning-mode.comment: No explicit plan-then-execute / read-only planning mode documented as of check 2026-06-05.

browser-computer-use.comment: No built-in browser/GUI driving for self-verification; capability comes only via external MCP servers if added.

operating-mode.comment: Interactive TUI chat; supervised agent with per-tool approval; autonomous when --yolo bypasses prompts. No inline completion or detached async runs.

inline-completion.comment: Terminal agent; no as-you-type tab autocomplete.

background-async-execution.comment: Sessions persist state and can be reattached by multiple clients, but no fire-and-forget detached task execution is documented.

persistent-memory.comment: Session-based conversation management with persistent state; sessions can be resumed across reconnections.

checkpoints-rollback.comment: No built-in checkpoint/rollback of the working tree documented as of check 2026-06-05; relies on user's own git.

git-integration.comment: Works inside git repos and can run git via its shell tool; described with git integration capabilities.

pr-creation.comment: No native PR-creation feature documented; could open PRs only by invoking the gh/git CLI through its shell tool. Marked null as of check 2026-06-05.

custom-rules-instructions.comment: Auto-loads an AGENTS.md initialization file (customizable via initialize_as) for project context/conventions.

hooks-extensibility.comment: Extensible via MCP servers and Agent Skills (SKILL.md packages); user-invocable skills appear in the command palette.

permission-model.comment: Prompts before tool execution by default; allowed_tools allow-list and --yolo configure/bypass approval; specific tools can be disabled.

self-host-on-prem.comment: Runs entirely on the user's machine; with a local model (Ollama/LM Studio) the whole pipeline can be self-hosted.

privacy-mode.comment: Crush itself does not train on code; runs locally and sends code only to the user's chosen provider. Telemetry is pseudonymous and opt-out. Effective privacy depends on the chosen model provider.

enterprise-controls.comment: Local CLI tool with no vendor backend; no SSO/audit/RBAC/VPC governance layer of its own.

pricing-model.comment: The tool is free; users pay only their own LLM provider (BYOK / usage on the provider side). No Charm subscription for Crush.

free-tier.comment: The agent is entirely free; only external model API usage costs money.

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: Tool is free (BYOK); 0 per RESEARCH.md since the free tool covers core use, with model API billed separately by the provider.

maturity.comment: Polished Charm UX and 25k+ GitHub stars, but pre-1.0 (v0.4.x) and rapidly evolving; solid but still changing as of check 2026-06-05.

community-size.comment: 25k+ GitHub stars and active Charm following within months of launch; sizable but below Aider/Claude Code scale.

extension-plugin-ecosystem.comment: Open MCP ecosystem plus Agent Skills (compatible with anthropics/skills community packages).

project-memory-files.comment: Auto-loads AGENTS.md by default (filename configurable via initialize_as).

memory-scopes.comment: Global user config ($HOME/.config/crush/crush.json) and per-project config (.crush.json / crush.json), plus project AGENTS.md.

scoped-rules-files.comment: AGENTS.md is an always-on project instruction file; no documented path/file-type/agent-requested conditional rule scoping.

auto-memory.comment: No autonomous cross-session memory writing documented; instruction files are manually authored. Session state persistence is not auto-memory.

skill-support.comment: Implements the open Agent Skills standard: SKILL.md skill folders discovered automatically.

skill-invocation.comment: user-invocable: true skills appear in the command palette (user:/project: prefixes); model auto-invocation is on by default and can be disabled per skill.

always-on-surface.comment: Agent Skills standard uses progressive disclosure: name + description discoverable, full SKILL.md body loaded on invocation.

skill-distribution.comment: Skills are folders dropped into skill directories (global ~/.config/crush/skills or project .crush/skills); community examples obtained via git (e.g. anthropics/skills). No curated marketplace.
